task_completion.description: Create a cross-functional swimlane layout with specific lanes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a swimlane diagram structure on the EdrawMax canvas with distinct lanes explicitly labeled for 'IT Security', 'Executive Management', and 'Legal/Compliance'.

task_completion.description: Incorporate decision points, action steps, and directional connectors mapping the incident response.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for flowchart shapes inside the swimlanes, specifically including rectangular action steps, diamond-shaped decision points, and directional arrows connecting the shapes.

task_completion.description: Apply a professional color theme to the flowchart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a cohesive color scheme applied to the flowchart shapes and swimlanes (e.g., distinct colors for different lanes or shape types, not just the default unstyled white/blank shapes).

task_completion.description: Create a second page in the EdrawMax document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the page navigation tabs (usually at the bottom of the EdrawMax workspace) to verify that a second page (e.g., 'Page 2') has been added to the document.

task_completion.description: Add an Executive Summary or Incident Impact Assessment to Page 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text elements on the second page containing an Executive Summary or Impact Assessment. The text should explicitly mention incident scope, affected systems, recovery time objective (RTO), and key lessons learned.

task_completion.description: Save the completed diagram to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the EdrawMax window title bar displaying 'ransomware_ir_flowchart.eddx', or a file explorer/terminal window showing the file successfully saved in the '/home/ga/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Wondershare EdrawMax GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the EdrawMax application open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas, rather than terminal commands generating an XML/EDDX file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used actual diagramming shapes rather than pasting a single static image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for selection handles around individual shapes, text boxes, or connectors, indicating the diagram was built using EdrawMax's native tools.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by creating a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal screenshots showing commands like 'touch /home/ga/ransomware_ir_flowchart.eddx' or 'echo > ...' without actual EdrawMax usage.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/ransomware_ir_flowchart.eddx'. The document must contain at least 2 pages. Page 1 must feature a cross-functional swimlane structure with decision diamonds. Page 2 must contain an Executive Summary or Incident Impact Assessment.
